tiles.question: What is the most important insight about reasoning?
tiles.answer: Edge case: A distributed system with 3 nodes where 1 is Byzantine-faulty. Iterative reasoning (try, challenge, refine) works at the node level to detect the faulty actor through repeated rounds of consensus checks. However, if all 3 nodes are on the same codebase with the same bug, iterative reasoning fails — the error compounds across all perspectives. The fix: diverse implementations (N-version programming) or external oracles that break the echo chamber. Final answer: Reasoning deepens through iteration, but correctness requires diversity of perspective and external grounding — iteration alone is necessary but not sufficient.

tiles.question: Lock reasoning engine test
tiles.answer: Tested the Lock iterative reasoning engine. Started a session, advanced through all 5 rounds, completed the full cycle. Available strategies include socratic, adversarial, decomposition, steel-man, red-team, metaphor, teaching, and bootstrap approaches. The engine successfully deepens reasoning through structured self-debate and automatically submits tiles on completion.
